name: Michigan Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Speech-Language Pathology
provider.parentOrganization: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)

address.description: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs, Bureau of Professional Licensing, P.O. Box 30670, Lansing, MI 48909

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Michigan SLP license cost?
Is Michigan part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Michigan has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

mainEntity.name: Does Michigan allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, with a valid Michigan license.

mainEntity.name: Does Michigan have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Michigan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
